message.content: ssh接続元のtmuxでもコピーできるようにOCS 52対応してください.

content.thinking:
The user wants to enable OSC 52 clipboard support so that copying works in tmux even when connected via SSH. OSC 52 is a terminal escape sequence that allows programs to set the system clipboard.

Let me first check what tmux and terminal configurations exist in this dotfiles repo.

content.text: まず、現在のtmuxとターミナル関連の設定を確認します。

file.content:
tmux = {
      enable = true;
      terminal = "tmux-256color";
      escapeTime = 0;
      historyLimit = 50000;
      mouse = true;
      baseIndex = 1;
      keyMode = "vi";
      prefix = "C-q";
      extraConfig = ''
        # Terminal overrides
        set -ag terminal-overrides ",xterm-256color:RGB"
        set -ag terminal-overrides ",ghostty:RGB"

        # Clipboard integration (OSC 52 — works over SSH + local Wayland)
        set -s set-clipboard on
        set -g allow-passthrough on
        set -ag terminal-overrides ",xterm-256color:Ms=\\E]52;c;%p2%s\\7"
        set -ag terminal-overrides ",ghostty:Ms=\\E]52;c;%p2%s\\7"

        # Copy: OSC 52 (always via set-clipboard on) + wl-copy (local only, silent fail over SSH)
        bind -T copy-mode-vi y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel "wl-copy 2>/dev/null || true"
        bind -T copy-mode-vi MouseDragEnd1Pane send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel "wl-copy 2>/dev/null || true"
        bind -T copy-mode MouseDragEnd1Pane send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel "wl-copy 2>/dev/null || true"

        # Paste: wl-paste (local) / tmux buffer fallback (SSH)
        bind v run "wl-paste -n 2>/dev/null | tmux load-buffer - ; tmux paste-buffer"
        bind -n MouseDown3Pane run "wl-paste -n 2>/dev/null | tmux load-buffer - ; tmux paste-buffer"

        # Pane base index
        setw -g pane-base-index 1

        # Split panes
        bind | split-window -h -c "#{pane_current_path}"
        bind - split-window -v -c "#{pane_current_path}"

        # Vim-like pane navigation
        bind h select-pane -L
        bind j select-pane -D
        bind k select-pane -U
        bind l select-pane -R

        # Reload config
        bind r source-file ~/.config/tmux/tmux.conf \; display "Config reloaded!"

        # tmuxcc agent dashboard popup
        bind a display-popup -E -w 80% -h 80% "tmuxcc || (echo 'tmuxcc failed. Press Enter to close.' && read)"

        # Beacon shortcuts (Ctrl+q b then key)
        bind b switch-client -T beacon
        bind -T beacon s display-popup -E -w 90% -h 80% "bash ~/.local/bin/beacon-status-popup.sh || (echo 'beacon status failed. Press Enter to close.' && read)"
        bind -T beacon w display-popup -E -w 90% -h 80% "bash ~/.local/bin/beacon-window-jump.sh || (echo 'beacon window jump failed. Press Enter to close.' && read)"
        bind -T beacon p display-popup -E -w 90% -h 80% "bash ~/.local/bin/beacon-pane-focus.sh || (echo 'beacon pane focus failed. Press Enter to close.' && read)"
        bind -T beacon c run-shell "beacon clean >/dev/null 2>&1"
        bind -T beacon q switch-client -T prefix
        bind -T beacon Escape switch-client -T prefix

        # Status bar
        set -g status on
        set -g status-interval 5
        set -g status-position bottom
        set -g status-justify left
        set -g status-style 'bg=#1e1e2e fg=#cdd6f4'

        # Left status - Session name
        set -g status-left-length 30
        set -g status-left '#[fg=#1e1e2e,bg=#89b4fa,bold] #S #[fg=#89b4fa,bg=#1e1e2e]'

        # Right status - Claude Code statusline + time
        set -g status-right-length 100

content.thinking:
Let me analyze the current state:

1. tmux already has some OSC 52 configuration:
   - `set -s set-clipboard on` 
   - `set -g allow-passthrough on`
   - Terminal overrides for `Ms` capability on `xterm-256color` and `ghostty`

2. The copy bindings use `wl-copy` which only works locally (Wayland), with `|| true` as fallback

3. Ghostty has `clipboard-read = ask` and `copy-on-select = true`

The issue is that the current setup relies on `wl-copy` for copying, which won't work over SSH. 

When `set -s set-clipboard on` is enabled, tmux automatically sends OSC 52 sequences to the terminal when text is copied in copy mode. The problem is that `copy-pipe-and-cancel` with `wl-copy` is intercepting this flow—it fails silently over SSH due to the `|| true` fallback, preventing the OSC 52 sequence from reaching the outer terminal. the buffer changes. So when I yank text in copy mode, `set-clipboard on` triggers tmux to send OSC 52 to the outer terminal. The `copy-pipe-and-cancel` command pipes to `wl-copy`, which fails silently over SSH, but the text still gets copied to the tmux buffer where `set-clipboard on` can handle it. The `Ms` override ensures the OSC 52 sequence is formatted correctly for the outer terminal to receive it.

The real issue might be the `TERM` value—if the outer SSH terminal isn't `xterm-256color` or `ghostty`, the `Ms` capability won't be set, so tmux won't know how to send OSC 52 properly. how to send OSC 52. If SSH is in between, the outer terminal from tmux's perspective would be whatever `TERM` the SSH connection sets.

Let me think about this differently. The user says "ssh接続元のtmuxでもコピーできるように" - they want copying to work on the tmux on the SSH remote, so that it reaches the local (SSH client) clipboard.

The chain is: Local terminal → SSH → Remote tmux → Application

For OSC 52 to work:
1. Remote tmux needs to emit OSC 52 (this is `set -s set-clipboard on` + `Ms` override)
2. SSH needs to pass the OSC 52 through (it does by default)
3. Local terminal needs to handle OSC 52 (Ghostty supports this)

The potential issues are around the `Ms` terminal override - when SSH'd in, the pseudo-terminal might report `xterm-256color` or whatever the SSH client sends, which could mismatch the configured overrides. Ghostty's clipboard handling should work fine with OSC 52 writes though.

Looking at the config, the core pieces are there - clipboard passthrough is enabled, the `Ms` override is set for common terminal types, and the copy bindings should work. I should probably add an override for `tmux-256color` in case of nested tmux, and verify the copy bindings are using the right approach. exclusively relying on `wl-copy`. But wait—I think the real issue might be whether `copy-pipe-and-cancel` actually triggers the `set-clipboard` mechanism and OSC 52 output the same way `copy-selection-and-cancel` does. That's an implementation detail I'm not entirely sure about. So the safest approach would be to either use `copy-selection-and-cancel` to guarantee OSC 52 gets sent, or use `copy-pipe-and-cancel` with an empty command to avoid the pipe failing over SSH while still relying on `set-clipboard on` to emit the escape sequence.

Now I'm realizing the actual problem might be on Ghostty's end—specifically whether it's configured to accept OSC 52 clipboard writes. I see `clipboard-read = ask` which controls reading the clipboard, but I need to check if there's a `clipboard-write` setting that controls whether Ghostty accepts incoming clipboard writes via OSC 52.

Looking at Ghostty's defaults, `clipboard-write` should be set to `allow` by default, so that's probably not the issue. The real question is whether the TERM variable is being set correctly when SSH'd into the remote machine—if it's not `xterm-256color` or `ghostty`, then the `Ms` capability override won't apply and OSC 52 won't work.

The key is that `allow-passthrough on` lets applications like nvim send their own OSC 52 sequences through tmux, while `set-clipboard on` handles tmux's copy mode. A more reliable approach would be to add a wildcard terminal override that catches any terminal type, rather than relying on specific TERM values.

Adding terminal overrides...

For the actual copy commands, the current setup should work well—`wl-copy` handles local Wayland clipboard while OSC 52 takes care of SSH sessions, so both paths are covered.

The key fixes are setting up a wildcard terminal override in tmux to catch any TERM value (especially important since SSH might forward `tmux-256color` or other variants), and explicitly enabling `clipboard-write` in Ghostty to ensure OSC 52 writes go through. Using a wildcard pattern in the tmux override is simpler and more robust than trying to account for every possible TERM value. I need to think through the nested tmux scenario more carefully. The user has tmux on their local machine, then SSH to a remote system that might also have tmux, and I need to ensure OSC 52 clipboard sequences pass through both layers. The `allow-passthrough on` setting is key for letting inner programs' escape sequences reach the outer terminal, and `set-clipboard on` on the remote tmux will generate OSC 52 that needs to propagate back through the local tmux to Ghostty. I'm realizing the flow: when remote tmux has `set-clipboard on`, it sends OSC 52 sequences that travel through SSH to local tmux, which then forwards them to Ghostty if local tmux also has `set-clipboard on` enabled. So the solution is ensuring both tmux instances have this setting configured.

Now I need to clarify what the user is actually asking for—they want clipboard to work when SSHing from another machine into their NixOS system, which means I should focus on configuring their local tmux setup to handle incoming OSC 52 sequences properly. I need to strengthen the OSC 52 configuration by adding a wildcard override for the `Ms` capability to handle any outer terminal, ensuring `set-clipboard` and `allow-passthrough` are both enabled, and explicitly setting Ghostty's clipboard permissions to allow write access.

content.text:
現在のtmux設定を確認しました。OSC 52の基本設定はありますが、SSH越しで確実に動作させるために2点修正が必要です。

**問題点:**
1. `Ms` (OSC 52) のterminal overrideが `xterm-256color` と `ghostty` のみ。SSH経由だと `tmux-256color` や `screen-256color` など他の `TERM` 値になりうるためカバーできていない
2. Ghosttyで `clipboard-write` が明示されておらず、OSC 52書き込み許可が暗黙的

input.old_string:
# Clipboard integration (OSC 52 — works over SSH + local Wayland)
        set -s set-clipboard on
        set -g allow-passthrough on
        set -ag terminal-overrides ",xterm-256color:Ms=\\E]52;c;%p2%s\\7"
        set -ag terminal-overrides ",ghostty:Ms=\\E]52;c;%p2%s\\7"
input.new_string:
# Clipboard integration (OSC 52 — works over SSH + local Wayland)
        set -s set-clipboard on
        set -g allow-passthrough on
        set -ag terminal-overrides ",*:Ms=\\E]52;c;%p2%s\\7"

input.old_string:
clipboard-read = ask
clipboard-paste-protection = true
copy-on-select = true
input.new_string:
clipboard-read = ask
clipboard-write = allow
clipboard-paste-protection = true
copy-on-select = true

content.text:
変更しました。

**tmux (`home/default.nix`)**
- `Ms` overrideを `xterm-256color` / `ghostty` 個別指定からワイルドカード `*` に変更。SSH越しで `TERM` が `tmux-256color` や `screen-256color` 等の場合もOSC 52が発行される

**Ghostty (`config/ghostty/config`)**
- `clipboard-write = allow` を明示追加。アプリケーション（tmux含む）からのOSC 52によるクリップボード書き込みを許可

これでSSH接続元のターミナル（Ghostty）がOSC 52をサポートしていれば、リモートのtmuxでコピーした内容がローカルのクリップボードに反映されます。反映には `sudo nixos-rebuild switch --flake .#ryobox` が必要です。

+clipboard-write = allow

 copy-on-select = true

         # Copy: OSC 52 (always via set-clipboard on) + wl-copy (local only, silent fail over SSH)
         bind -T copy-mode-vi y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel "wl-copy 2>/dev/null || true"

input.command:
git add config/ghostty/config home/default.nix && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(tmux): use wildcard Ms override for reliable OSC 52 over SSH

The previous terminal-overrides only covered xterm-256color and ghostty,
so OSC 52 clipboard writes failed when the outer TERM was tmux-256color
or screen-256color (common over SSH). Wildcard ensures OSC 52 works
regardless of the connecting terminal type. Also explicitly allow
clipboard-write in Ghostty for OSC 52 acceptance.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
